mappings.rationale: Directly covers photography as an art, including photographic equipment, techniques, and kinds of photography — the subject of section D.
Covers the history of drawing, painting, and printmaking in Western art, directly relevant to sections A.4, B.6.a, C.4.
Addresses theoretical questions about visual arts including design elements, representation, and iconography — topics central to this section.
Covers modern developments in painting and drawing including nonrepresentational painting (section B.5.b.ii), directly relevant to Western painting history.
Covers contemporary painting, drawing, and photography practices, relevant to the most recent developments in these arts.
Renaissance painting and drawing are central to Western art history, directly relevant to sections B.6.a and A.4.
Dada and Surrealist painting, drawing, and collage expanded artistic techniques, relevant to Western painting history and nonrepresentational painting.
Avant-garde movements transformed painting and drawing, relevant to the section's treatment of Western painting history (section B.6.a).
Futurist painting is a significant movement in Western painting history, relevant to section B.6.a.
Ancient Egyptian painting including mural and cave painting is relevant to the section's treatment of painting on fixed objects (section B.5.a.i) and non-Western painting (section B.6.b).
Christian painting including devotional painting, manuscript illumination, and stained glass are directly relevant to sections B.5.a, B.5.b.i, and B.4.f.
Hieroglyphic art combines drawing and writing, relevant to the section's treatment of calligraphy and its relationship to drawing and painting.
Relevant to the section's treatment of photographic equipment and techniques, particularly in the context of scientific photography (section D.3.b).
Classical Greek and Roman painting traditions including mural painting and mosaic are relevant to sections B.4.c, B.5.a.i, and Western painting history.
The Harlem Renaissance included significant developments in American painting and drawing, relevant to Western painting history.
Relevant to the section's treatment of elements of design and principles of composition in drawing and painting (sections A.1 and B.1).
Understanding light is fundamental to painting and photography, relevant to the section's treatment of photographic exposure and artistic use of light.
Relevant to the section's treatment of photojournalism and photo reportage as a kind of functional photography (section D.3.b).
Typography relates to printmaking and the artistic use of type, relevant to the section's treatment of printmaking media and techniques (section C).
Prehistoric cave painting is the earliest form of painting, directly relevant to the section's treatment of painting on fixed objects (section B.5.a.i) and primitive painting (section B.5.c).
Relevant to the section's treatment of primitive and folk painting (section B.5.c) and drawing traditions across cultures.
Romantic painting including landscape painting is a major period in Western painting history, relevant to section B.6.a.
The Decadent movement influenced painting and illustration in the late 19th century, relevant to Western painting history.
